---
title: "Workshop 02 - Field Data Collection"
subtitle: "Methods and Protocols for Arboreal Surveys"
author: "John Smith"
date: 2024-06-01
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    footer: "ARBO-WATCH — Workshop 02"
---

## Overview

- Field safety and survey protocols
- Point count and transect methods
- Recording and validating field data

---

## Field Safety

- Always work in pairs in the field
- Notify base camp of your location and expected return
- Carry a first aid kit and emergency contact list

---

## Point Count Method

- Stand at a fixed point for a set time (e.g. 10 minutes)
- Record all arboreal species seen or heard within a radius
- Repeat at multiple points across the study area

---

## Transect Surveys

- Walk a fixed route at a slow pace
- Record species, distance, and canopy height
- Use GPS to log the transect path

---

## Data Recording

```{.r}
# Example field data structure
# site, date, observer, species, count, canopy_height_m, notes
```

- Use standardised field sheets
- Double-check species identifications in the field
- Enter data into the digital system within 24 hours

---

## Summary

- Consistent protocols ensure comparable data across sites
- GPS tracking links observations to spatial context
- Cleaned field data feeds directly into R analysis workflows
